# GitFlix

> Turn any public GitHub repository into a cinematic documentary video.

GitFlix analyses a repository's commit history and generates a seven-scene
animated film: contributors become characters, big commit weeks become plot
twists, and the codebase's story is narrated by an LLM and set to music.
No data is stored — each generation is ephemeral and streamed in real time.

## How to use

Paste a public GitHub URL (e.g. https://github.com/react/react), choose
a tone (epic, documentary, or casual), and click Generate Film. The result
is a ~2.5-minute video you can watch in the browser or export as MP4.

## Scenes

- S01 Origin: repository name, creation date, and first contributor
- S02 Cast: top contributors with roles (hero, ghost, late joiner, backbone)
- S03 The Rise: commit volume over time as a donut/arc chart
- S04 Plot Twist: the busiest week in the repo's history
- S05 Ghost Files: files that haven't been touched in 6+ months
- S06 Hero Commit: the single largest diff ever merged
- S07 Finale: summary stats — total commits, contributors, days active

## Tones

- epic: dramatic, high-stakes narration
- documentary: calm, factual, David Attenborough-style
- casual: lighthearted and conversational

## Technical limits

- Public GitHub repositories only (private repos require a token on the backend)
- Large repositories (10,000+ commits) may take longer than the ~60s average
- Export to MP4 requires a separate render server (hosted on Render free tier)

## Source

- Live site: https://gitflix.netlify.app
- GitHub repository: https://github.com/cr2007/GitFlix
